{{Infobox ancient site | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Okameishi Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Stone sarcophagus File:Okameishi Kofun, sendou.jpg|Entrourage looking towards burial chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳}}''|方墳}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Okameishi Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Stone sarcophagus File:Okameishi Kofun, sendou.jpg|Entrourage looking towards burial chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue ware]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue pottery]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue pottery]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Okameishi Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Stone sarcophagus File:Okameishi Kofun, sendou.jpg|Entrourage looking towards burial chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue pottery]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Okameishi Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Stone sarcophagus File:Okameishi Kofun, sendou.jpg|Entrourage looking towards burial chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Okameishi Kofun}} *[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2592.html Tondabayashi City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.minamikawachi.jp/institution_page.php?eid=00086 Minamikawachi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue pottery]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Okameishi Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Stone sarcophagus File:Okameishi Kofun, sendou.jpg|Entrourage looking towards burial chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Okameishi Kofun | native_name =お亀石古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Okameishi Kofun, entrance.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Okameishi Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.early 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|44.091|N|135|35|55.170|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Okameishi Kofun'''|お亀石古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] square-shaped [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakano neighborhood of the city of [[Tondabayashi, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Okameishi Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style tumulus with 21-meter square sides. It is located on a ridge that protrudes southeast from the Habino Hills at an elevation of 98 meters. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[tuff]] from [[Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[Burial goods]] included shards of [[Sue pottery]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[roof tile]]s from the [[Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Okameishi Kofun, sekkan.jpg|Stone sarcophagus File:Okameishi Kofun, sendou.jpg|Entrourage looking towards burial chamber </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Okameishi Kofun}} *[https://www.city.tondabayashi.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2592.html Tondabayashi City home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.minamikawachi.jp/institution_page.php?eid=00086 Minamikawachi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}
Okameishi Kofun

No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found at the site.The tumulus is orientated to the south and has a horizontal grantite-lined [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] containing a house-shaped sarcophagus.The sarcophagus is made of white [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] from [[:en:Mount Nijō|Mount Nijō]] and has a stone lid with six protrusions for hanging by ropes. As the lid has been exposed for a long time it has weathered and resembles the shape of a turtle, hence the name "Kameishi". An [[archaeological excavation]] in 2002 revealed that the entourage was originally closed with a river stone. [[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] included shards of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tondabayashi Station|Tondabayashi Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Nagano Line|Kintetsu Nagano Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2002 collectively with the nearby [[:en:Shindō temple ruins|Shindō temple ruins]] and the Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/3348 |title=新堂廃寺跡 附オガンジ池瓦窯跡 お亀石古墳|trans-title=Shindo temple ruins with Oganji-ike tile kiln ruins and Okameishi Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>

[[:en:Burial goods|Burial goods]] included shards of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery, which date the tumulus to the first half of the 7th century. An unusual feature is that many flat [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s from the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] are stacked up around the sarcophagus forming a retaining wall. The tiles are the same type as the [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]-style roof tiles used at the Shindō temple ruins located to the southeast, and may indicate that the person buried in this time had some close relationship with that temple.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
